summary: Tom Bradley led a post-incident review of the Detect outage from March 10th, which caused approximately six hours of no threat monitoring for customers due to a cascading failure in the event ingestion pipeline caused by a single point of failure. Tom explained the deployed fixes including redundant processing nodes and a circuit breaker pattern, while Megan noted upcoming product improvements including a status indicator and a potential heartbeat alert feature. Diana highlighted customer concerns about lack of visibility, particularly among enterprise and mid-renewal accounts, and the team aligned on next steps for customer communications. Key action items include Tom delivering a formal post-incident report, Megan evaluating roadmap prioritization, and Diana coordinating customer calls.
actionItems: Tom Bradley: Deliver the full technical PIR document (root cause, fix, and prevention measures) to Diana by tomorrow morning
Diana Reeves: Adapt PIR into customer-facing language and send to affected accounts by end of week, coordinating calls with enterprise and mid-renewal accounts
Megan Lawson: Review roadmap by end of week to assess feasibility of pulling forward the heartbeat alert feature
Tom Bradley: Make himself available for technical deep-dive calls with up to three or four enterprise customers flagged by Diana

keyMoments.text: Tom confirms customers had six hours of no threat monitoring alerts due to a single point of failure in the ingestion pipeline
keyMoments.speaker: Tom Bradley

keyMoments.text: Diana flags that customers feel like they're flying blind with Detect and that this incident reinforced that concern
keyMoments.speaker: Diana Reeves

keyMoments.text: Megan introduces the heartbeat alert feature concept, which would proactively notify customers if telemetry stops being received — Diana responds it would be huge
keyMoments.speaker: Megan Lawson

keyMoments.text: Diana notes that several enterprise accounts mid-renewal will want calls, not just an email, raising renewal risk
keyMoments.speaker: Diana Reeves
